_meta.framework_note: Repo vendors a partial CW SpecKit (.specify/templates present) but check-prerequisites.sh could not resolve CW_SPECIFY_HOME in this env; FEATURE_DIR and AVAILABLE_DOCS were resolved manually (all design docs present). Tasks emitted directly under specs/{spec-id}/ following the sibling completed spec 2026-05-26-gemini-failover-design convention. Test harness is the Node built-in runner (package.json test = 'node --test src/**/__tests__/**/*.test.js'), NOT Jest — there is no npm run test:unit/test:integration/test:smoke/type-check; gate commands are mapped to the runner that actually exists (see T-FINAL composed_of notes). 3 tests already exist (askService-sse, gemini, geminiModelRotator); AC-1..5 follow the gemini.test.js Module._load precedent (research.md DISC-1).

fields.id: stable task id; T-P{phase}.{step} matches plan §6 / Phase 2 Task-Planning table; T-PRE-*, T-GATE, T-DOC-GATE, T-MEM-CAPTURE, T-FINAL are framework gates
fields.phase: pre-impl | p0 | p1 | gate | p2 | p3 | p4 | verify
fields.description: single-sentence imperative — what this task does
fields.parallel: true if this task can run concurrently with siblings in the same phase (different target_file or independent code path)
fields.target_file: absolute-from-repo-root path of the file edited or created — required when parallel=true
fields.depends_on: array of task ids that must be completed before this one
fields.exit_criteria: short string copied from the plan/spec validation column
fields.plan_ref: back-reference to the plan/spec FR(s) this task implements
fields.fr_ref: spec functional-requirement ids this task satisfies
fields.ship_gated: true if this task is NOT pre-committed and is decided by the hard gate (T-GATE) from Phase 0 evidence

critical_requirements._warning: spec.md has NO `critical_requirements` YAML frontmatter. The values below were INFERRED by /tasks from spec/plan content so generation could proceed. RECOMMENDED: add a critical_requirements frontmatter block to spec.md (type/portal/routes/permissions/data_scoping/high_risk_apis/ui_changes/ui_complexity/database_changes) and re-run /tasks to make this authoritative rather than inferred.
critical_requirements.permissions: none — single-user Electron desktop app; no auth/access-control surface (plan Phase 0.2 SKIPPED)
critical_requirements.data_scoping: in-memory runtime state only (session pointers, sessionGeneration counter, dropped-chunk counters, per-side reconnectState); NO DB schema, NO env vars, NO migrations (plan Phase 0.5 SKIPPED)
high_risk_apis: Deepgram realtime STT WebSocket (@deepgram/sdk; deepgram.js sendRealtimeInput/close/onclose/onerror)
ws socket lifecycle (readyState, close codes/reasons — the 1000/'client' filter is load-bearing, D2)
Renderer ScriptProcessorNode audio graph + WASM/Rust AEC (listenCapture.js — the AEC/mic/loopback heart; refactor can silently break audio)
Hot audio IPC path ~10 msgs/s/channel (listen:sendMicAudio / listen:sendSystemAudio — additive generation field must not break older renderer builds)
20-minute renewal timer + keep-alive timer (sttService renewSessions/initializeSttSessions reschedule-after-throw bug)
critical_requirements.pre_checks: T-PRE-PATTERN always; T-PRE-UI (ui_changes=minor + UI keywords: panel/button/status/layout for the Reconnecting…/Resume affordance); T-PRE-SCHEMA SKIPPED (database_changes=false, no schema/migration/new-model keywords, plan Phase 0.5 SKIPPED)

summary.phasing_note: Spec §5 + plan Phase 2 Task-Planning: ship Phase 0 (instrumentation) + Phase 1 (guarded send) FIRST, then a HARD APPROVAL GATE (T-GATE). Phases 2-4 are NOT pre-committed — they are decided at T-GATE from real-usage [stt-lifecycle] logs (quickstart §5 exit criteria). Every Phase 2/3/4 task carries ship_gated:true. Phase 3 reconnect lands ONLY if Phase 0 surfaces a non-'client' close (today's logs show none — spec §2.4). The idempotent-start fix (T-P2.2, FR-2.1) is the likely first pull-forward regardless.
summary.test_strategy_note: AC-1 automated NOW (Phase 1, before FR-1.1 — Constitution Test-First). AC-2..5 authored alongside their gated phases. AC-6..9 are MANUAL smoke/regression (live Deepgram socket lifecycle is not deterministically CI-automatable; E2E FORBIDDEN per research.md §4 gate). All automated units use node:test + Module._load mocking (gemini.test.js precedent).
summary.parallelism_note: Within a phase, tasks with distinct target_file run in parallel. sttService.js is touched in p0/p1/p3/p4 so those edits are sequential against each other; listenCapture.js is touched in p0/p2. Cross-phase order is strictly gated by depends_on.
summary.deviation_note: No tasks-template T-VERIFY-* individual tasks are emitted (replaced by the composite T-FINAL per /tasks step 7). T-MEM-CAPTURE IS emitted because critical_requirements.type=bugfix (step 6.8). typecheck/db-validate/contract/e2e/a11y are SKIPPED inside T-FINAL with reasons (vanilla JS, no DB, no HTTP API, no automatable browser E2E, low UI complexity).

tasks.description: Pre-implementation pattern-compliance check via the pattern registry: bracketed-tag console logging ([SttService]/[ListenService] + new [stt-lifecycle]), {success,error} IPC result shape, single-live-session-per-side invariant (no failover — D1), monotonic generation reuse (FR-0.2 → FR-2.4 same concept, not two), and the mandatory reconnect close-filter !(1000 && 'client') (D2).
tasks.concurrency_note: No file contention (agent-check, edits nothing). May be dispatched concurrently with T-PRE-UI by the orchestrator; parallel is set false only to satisfy the 'parallel⇒target_file' validation rule.
tasks.verify: Pattern check completes without FAIL status; warnings allowed
tasks.exit_criteria: Pattern check report Status: PASS or PASS-WITH-WARN
tasks.plan_ref: plan Phase 0.7.5 Prior Lessons (D2 reconnect filter); /tasks step 5

tasks.description: Pre-implementation UI preflight scan for the Minor-UI surface (FR-3.4): the 'Reconnecting…'/'Reconnected' status text reuses the existing update-status string channel, and the exhaustion state adds a one-click 'Resume' affordance (Stop→Listen). Confirm no new component-library component, no design-token surface, no header-width impact (MainHeader.js untouched).
tasks.concurrency_note: No file contention (agent-check, edits nothing). May be dispatched concurrently with T-PRE-PATTERN by the orchestrator; parallel is set false only to satisfy the 'parallel⇒target_file' validation rule.
tasks.verify: UI preflight scan completes; no BLOCKER findings on the status-line / Resume affordance
tasks.exit_criteria: ui-preflight report generated; no BLOCKER findings (plan Phase 0.4 = Minor, SKIPPED at design time — this is the implementation-time scan)
tasks.plan_ref: plan Phase 0.4 (Minor UI); spec FR-3.4

tasks.description: Introduce the monotonic session-generation primitive in SttService: add this.sessionGeneration (init 0), this.droppedChunkCounters={my:0,their:0}, this.gapEpisode={my:{active,count,fromGen},their:{...}}, this.isClosing=false. Increment sessionGeneration once at the top of initializeSttSessions (the ONE concept FR-2.4 reuses — do not invent a second generation). No behavioral change beyond field creation + the increment.
tasks.exit_criteria: Fields present; sessionGeneration strictly increases on each initializeSttSessions (INV-2); app still transcribes (no regression)
tasks.plan_ref: spec FR-0.2; data-model Entity 1; plan Phase 0.7 risk row 'Phase 0 generation'

tasks.description: Add [stt-lifecycle]-tagged, timestamped transition logs (LifecycleLogRecord shape from contracts §5) at: initializeSttSessions begin/end, closeSessions begin/end, renewSessions begin/end, and each socket open/close carrying {code, reason, readyState}. Stamp the current sessionGeneration (T-P0.1) on every line. Logging only — no behavioral change.
tasks.exit_criteria: Each listed transition emits exactly one [stt-lifecycle] line with generation; socket close lines show {code,reason,readyState} (proves 'client' vs non-'client' for the gate)
tasks.plan_ref: spec FR-0.1, FR-0.2; contracts LifecycleLogRecord

tasks.description: Add the renderer-side [stt-lifecycle] transition logs + orphan-creation detector in listenCapture.js: log startCapture begin/end and stopCapture begin/end; emit a single 'orphan-processor-detected' warning when startCapture is about to connect a processor while a previous processor global (audioProcessor/systemAudioProcessor) is still non-null (detects the FR-3.2b orphan path). Logging only — no behavioral change.
tasks.exit_criteria: startCapture/stopCapture begin/end lines appear; a double-Start reproduces an 'orphan-processor-detected' warning in the console (validates A1 instrumentation)
tasks.plan_ref: spec FR-0.1, FR-0.4; spec §3.2

tasks.description: Add the dropped-chunk counter + throttled gap-episode summary seam in sendMicAudioContent/sendSystemAudioContent: when the session is null, increment droppedChunkCounters[side] and emit ONE throttled 'dropped-chunks-summary' line per gap episode (e.g. '[stt-lifecycle] dropped 7 mic chunks while session inactive (gen 3→null)'), NOT one per chunk. In Phase 0 the throw still happens after counting (Phase 1 removes the throw) — this is the measurement seam Phase 1 formalizes.
tasks.exit_criteria: A Stop/Start burst produces exactly one summary line per gap episode with an accurate count; no per-chunk spam from the counter itself
tasks.plan_ref: spec FR-0.3; contracts GapEpisodeState; data-model Entity 1

tasks.description: Write the FAILING AC-1 unit test FIRST (Constitution Test-First) at src/features/listen/stt/__tests__/sttService-lifecycle.test.js using node:test + Module._load interception (mirror gemini.test.js, NOT askService-sse.test.js — research.md DISC-1): stub require('electron'), require('../../common/ai/factory') (createSTT → scripted fake {sendRealtimeInput,close,readyState}), modelStateService, and ../../../window/windowManager (DISC-2). Assert: with mySttSession/theirSttSession=null, sendMicAudioContent/sendSystemAudioContent RESOLVE (no throw) and the dropped-chunk counter increments. Test must FAIL against current throwing code.
tasks.exit_criteria: node --test src/features/listen/stt/__tests__/sttService-lifecycle.test.js runs and FAILS on the null-session assertion (red) before T-P1.2
tasks.plan_ref: spec AC-1, §10 Q4→B; research.md DISC-1/2; quickstart §2

tasks.description: Guarded send (FR-1.1): change sendMicAudioContent (:547) and sendSystemAudioContent (:575) to NOT throw when the session is null — return a benign {success:true, dropped:true}, drop the chunk, and count it via the T-P0.4 seam. Mirror the macOS native-loop skip-don't-crash semantics (sttService.js:687/:699). This makes AC-1 (T-P1.1) go green.
tasks.exit_criteria: AC-1 passes (no throw, counter++); a Stop/Start burst produces ZERO 'User/Their STT session not active' lines (the headline Phase 1 outcome)
tasks.plan_ref: spec FR-1.1, FR-1.2; contracts AudioSendResult/SendAudioContent; data-model INV-3; EDGE_CASES E1/E2

tasks.description: Regression-guard the IPC error contract (FR-1.3): confirm handleSendSystemAudioContent (:65) and the _createHandler-wrapped handleSendMicAudioContent still return {success:false, error} on GENUINE errors (not the benign null-session drop), preserving commit 4882438's non-fatal IPC behavior. Add a test case to sttService-lifecycle.test.js asserting a thrown non-null-session error still surfaces {success:false}.
tasks.exit_criteria: Genuine-error path still yields {success:false,error}; null-session path yields {success:true,dropped:true}; no regression to 4882438
tasks.plan_ref: spec FR-1.3, decision D5; research.md DISC-4

tasks.description: HARD APPROVAL GATE (spec §5 Clarify Q1→A): collect real-usage [stt-lifecycle] logs from shipped Phase 0+1 and evaluate the quickstart §5 exit criteria — (a) do the 'not active'/dropped-chunk episodes correlate with socket:close {reason:'client'} Stop events? (b) are there any 'orphan-processor-detected' warnings (validates A1, decides Phase 2 depth)? (c) is there ANY non-'client' close (decides whether Phase 3 reconnect is load-bearing vs pure hardening)? Decide which of Phases 2/3/4 proceed. CHECKPOINT — not a code change.
tasks.verify: Gate decision recorded: per-phase go/no-go for Phase 2 (idempotent-start likely pull-forward), Phase 3 (only if a non-'client' close was observed), Phase 4 (as renewal evidence warrants)
tasks.block_on: Phase 0 evidence not yet collected from real usage, OR decision not recorded
tasks.exit_criteria: Documented go/no-go for each of Phases 2/3/4 from Phase 0 logs; spec §5 + quickstart §5
tasks.plan_ref: spec §5 Delivery sequencing; plan Phase 2 Task-Planning 'HARD GATE'; research.md §6
tasks.note: All downstream p2/p3/p4 tasks have ship_gated:true and are blocked on this checkpoint. They remain pending until the gate approves their phase.

tasks.description: Replace the four module-level capture globals (audioContext/audioProcessor/systemAudioContext/systemAudioProcessor) with a single tracked resource model in listenCapture.js: a `resources` Set of {kind:'context'|'processor'|'stream', ref} plus a monotonic `captureToken` and a `captureGeneration` (FR-2.2). Keep the audio-graph WIRING identical — change ONLY ownership/tracking. Remove the in-function global write in setupMicProcessing (:341) so ownership lives in exactly one place (research.md DISC-3).
tasks.exit_criteria: Every context/processor/stream created in a capture is registered in `resources`; setupMicProcessing no longer double-assigns the global (DISC-3); audio still transcribes
tasks.plan_ref: spec FR-2.2; data-model Entity 2 INV-6; research.md DISC-3; plan Phase 0.7 'Phase 2 capture core'

tasks.description: Idempotent + cancelable startCapture (FR-2.1, FR-2.3) — the likely first gate pull-forward: startCapture first runs full teardown of any existing capture, then captures its captureToken locally; after each await (getUserMedia/getDisplayMedia/AEC load) and BEFORE connecting processors it re-checks the token — on mismatch it tears down what it built and connects NOTHING (INV-5/INV-7). Make stopCapture disconnect/close EVERY member of `resources` and advance captureToken (INV-6). Covers EDGE_CASES E9/E10.
tasks.exit_criteria: Stop-during-in-flight-Start and double-Start leave ZERO orphaned processors (no 'orphan-processor-detected' warnings under AC-6 mashing); stopCapture tears down all tracked resources
tasks.plan_ref: spec FR-2.1, FR-2.3; data-model INV-5/6/7; EDGE_CASES E9/E10

tasks.description: Generation-stamped audio, main-side backstop (FR-2.4, FR-2.5 Q5→A): stamp captureGeneration on every chunk sent via sendMic/SystemAudioContent (renderer) and thread the optional `generation` field through the audio IPC payload (additive — featureBridge.js :149/:151, preload.js :305-306; absent ⇒ stale-safe default, EDGE_CASES E15). In sttService send handlers, DROP any chunk whose generation ≠ active sessionGeneration. closeSession makes late audio inert by bumping the generation (FR-2.5); the renderer capture-stopped ack is NOT implemented.
tasks.exit_criteria: A chunk stamped gen N is dropped when active gen is N+1 (AC-2); payload without generation does not crash the handler (E15); IPC stays additive/backward-compatible
tasks.plan_ref: spec FR-2.4, FR-2.5; contracts AudioChunkPayload; data-model Entity 3 INV-8; EDGE_CASES E1/E15

tasks.description: Ordered teardown + Done hygiene in listenService.js (FR-2.5, FR-2.6): make closeSession (:231) bump the session generation + null the pointers so late audio is dropped by generation (no IPC-ack race); review initializeSession finally (:205-209) so the renderer is not told to 'start' on an init-failure path. Verify Done (:80-84) cannot leave capture running — if reachable without a preceding Stop (per MainHeader header state table), route Done through the same teardown (EDGE_CASES E13).
tasks.exit_criteria: Stop is correctness-by-generation (not ack-timing); Done verified safe or routed through teardown; init-failure path no longer emits a misleading 'start'
tasks.plan_ref: spec FR-2.5, FR-2.6; data-model header state machine; research.md D-R6; EDGE_CASES E13

tasks.description: Write the AC-2 generation-drop unit test in sttService-lifecycle.test.js (node:test + Module._load): assert a chunk carrying generation N is ignored (dropped, counted) when the active sessionGeneration is N+1, and that a payload with no generation field is treated as the stale-safe default without throwing (E15). Author alongside FR-2.4 per Clarify Q4→B.
tasks.exit_criteria: AC-2 passes: gen-N chunk dropped at active gen N+1; missing-generation payload safe (E15)
tasks.plan_ref: spec AC-2, §10 Q4→B; EDGE_CASES E1/E15

tasks.description: Deep liveness (FR-3.1): expose a readiness probe on the Deepgram wrapper (return ws.readyState and/or isOpen() === (readyState===OPEN)) per contracts SttSessionWrapper, and change isSessionActive() (:743) + the startCapture precheck (listenCapture.js:520) to consider socket health, not just non-null. A dead-but-non-null socket (readyState !== 1) MUST report inactive (EDGE_CASES E6). Reconnect for the absent Linux `their` side must no-op (E12).
tasks.exit_criteria: isSessionActive() returns false when a session object exists but readyState !== OPEN (AC-5); Linux missing-their path does not crash (E12)
tasks.plan_ref: spec FR-3.1; contracts SttSessionWrapper/WsReadyState; data-model Entity 5; EDGE_CASES E6/E12
tasks.ship_condition: Phase 3 lands only if T-GATE found a non-'client' close (spec §2.4 shows none today — otherwise this is hardening)

tasks.description: Per-side same-session reconnect (FR-3.2, D1/D2; Clarify Q2 values): add per-side reconnectState (data-model Entity 4) and wrap the onclose/onerror callbacks so that, ONLY when !(code===1000 && reason==='client') (isDeliberateClose false — D2), the affected side ('my' or 'their') is recreated via createSTT with identical provider/model/handlers/config. Backoff base 250ms ×2, cap 4s, ±20% jitter (~0.25/0.5/1/2/4/4s), max 6 attempts; reduce Deepgram per-attempt open-timeout 10s→5s during reconnect (deepgram.js:63). Atomic swap on success — assign new pointer THEN close the dead one, never a transient null (FR-3.3, INV-1, EDGE_CASES E3/E4/E16); never two concurrent reconnects for one side (VAL-2).
tasks.exit_criteria: onclose({1000,'client'}) ⇒ NO reconnect; onclose({1011}) ⇒ reconnect that side only (AC-4); no overlapping sockets / no transient null (FR-3.3); reconnect never fires on a Stop or renewal hand-off
tasks.plan_ref: spec FR-3.2, FR-3.3, D1, D2; contracts ReconnectState/ReconnectPolicy/isDeliberateClose; data-model Entity 4; EDGE_CASES E3/E4/E16; plan Phase 0.7.5 lesson (0.97)
tasks.ship_condition: Phase 3 lands only if T-GATE found a non-'client' close

tasks.description: Reconnect status + exhaustion UX (FR-3.4; Clarify Q3→A): emit update-status 'Reconnecting…'/'Reconnected' (reuse existing string channel — no new IPC) during reconnect so there is no silent dead air; on exhaustion (6 attempts) show a visible status with a one-click 'Resume' that performs Stop→Listen. Capture keeps running and any still-live side keeps transcribing (relies on FR-1.1). NO auto-restart (avoids restart loops on a persistent fault). Update docs/diagrams now that reconnect exists (see T-DOC-GATE for the doc flip).
tasks.exit_criteria: Reconnecting/exhausted states surface to the UI; exhaustion shows Resume (no auto-loop); live side keeps transcribing (AC-8 manual)
tasks.plan_ref: spec FR-3.4, §12; contracts ReconnectUiStatus; data-model Entity 4; EDGE_CASES E5
tasks.ship_condition: Phase 3 lands only if T-GATE found a non-'client' close

tasks.description: Write AC-4 + AC-5 unit tests in sttService-lifecycle.test.js (node:test + Module._load with a scripted fake socket exposing readyState and onclose): AC-4 — onclose({code:1000,reason:'client'}) triggers NO reconnect; onclose({code:1011}) triggers a reconnect for that side only, with the atomic swap (E16). AC-5 — isSessionActive() returns false when the session object exists but readyState !== OPEN (E6). Author alongside Phase 3 per Clarify Q4→B.
tasks.exit_criteria: AC-4 and AC-5 pass; the D2 close-filter and deep-liveness are covered by automated units
tasks.plan_ref: spec AC-4, AC-5, §10 Q4→B; EDGE_CASES E3/E4/E6/E16
tasks.ship_condition: Phase 3 lands only if T-GATE found a non-'client' close

tasks.description: Renewal hardening (FR-4.1, FR-4.2, FR-4.3) in sttService.js: (a) if initializeSttSessions under renewal rejects after one createSTT already resolved, CLOSE the resolved socket (no leak — :469 Promise.all partial); (b) move the renew-timer reschedule so a FAILED renewal always re-arms itself (today the reschedule at :483-491 sits after the throw point, so one failure silently stops all future renewals) — reschedule in a finally/catch unless isClosing; (c) generation/isClosing-guard the assign so a closeSessions landing during the renewal await cannot resurrect sessions or re-arm timers (FR-4.3, INV-4, EDGE_CASES E8).
tasks.exit_criteria: Renew partial failure closes the opened socket; a failed renewal reschedules itself; a concurrent Stop aborts the in-flight renewal with no ghost session/timer (E8)
tasks.plan_ref: spec FR-4.1, FR-4.2, FR-4.3; data-model INV-4; EDGE_CASES E7/E8; plan Phase 0.7 'Phase 4 renewal'
tasks.ship_condition: Phase 4 lands as renewal evidence warrants (T-GATE decision)

tasks.description: Resolve SOCKET_OVERLAP_MS (FR-4.4) and write AC-3: EITHER make SOCKET_OVERLAP_MS (:21,:536) actually dual-route audio during the window, OR remove it and document that hand-off is instantaneous (it currently does neither — spec §3.5). Then write AC-3 in sttService-lifecycle.test.js: a mocked partial createSTT failure under renewSessions ⇒ the resolved socket's close() IS called AND a renewal retry is scheduled (FR-4.1/FR-4.2). Author alongside Phase 4 per Clarify Q4→B.
tasks.exit_criteria: SOCKET_OVERLAP_MS either dual-routes or is removed+documented; AC-3 passes (resolved socket close() called + retry scheduled)
tasks.plan_ref: spec FR-4.4, AC-3, §3.5; EDGE_CASES E7
tasks.ship_condition: Phase 4 lands as renewal evidence warrants (T-GATE decision)

tasks.description: Documentation reconciliation — identify and update docs affected by the implementation. If Phase 3 (reconnect) shipped, FLIP the 'no reconnect / manual Stop→Listen' claims in docs/AUDIO_AND_STT.md §5, docs/runbook/AUDIO_TROUBLESHOOTING.md §4, and docs/diagrams/08-stt-session-lifecycle.mmd to describe the same-session per-side reconnect (D1/D2). If only Phase 0+1 shipped, document the [stt-lifecycle] instrumentation + guarded-send behavior and leave the no-reconnect claim intact. Update ARCHITECTURE.md STT section if session-lifecycle behavior changed.
tasks.exit_criteria: Affected docs reconciled to the actually-shipped phase set; report Status: PASS
tasks.plan_ref: spec §11 docs row; quickstart §6; /tasks step 6
tasks.note: depends_on lists the last task of each shippable phase; the EFFECTIVE last-implementation task is whichever phases the gate approved. T-DOC-GATE runs once the shipped set is complete (Phase 1-only ships gate on T-P1.2).

tasks.description: Memory capture for bugfix. Validates that a type:bug memory entry was authored for this fix. The gate_check resolves the entry path from spec.md meta.memory_entry (primary) or MEMORY_ENTRY_PATH env (fallback per Q3), then invokes memory-schema-enforcer/verify.sh against it. Pass requires all 6 schema rules satisfied. EMITTED because critical_requirements.type=bugfix (/tasks step 6.8). NOTE: a prior 0.97-confidence memory entry already exists (stt-session-not-active-is-stopstart-race.md, cited in plan Phase 0.7.5); this gate ensures the as-shipped fix's root cause/symptoms/affected-files are captured/updated.
tasks.gate_check: bash -c 'MEM_PATH="${MEMORY_ENTRY_PATH:-$(grep -E "^memory_entry:" specs/${SPEC_DIR}/spec.md | sed -E "s/^memory_entry:[[:space:]]*//")}"; if [ -z "$MEM_PATH" ] || [ "$MEM_PATH" = "null" ]; then echo "[T-MEM-CAPTURE] memory entry path not set (neither meta.memory_entry in spec.md nor MEMORY_ENTRY_PATH env)" >&2; exit 1; fi; bash .claude/skills/memory-schema-enforcer/verify.sh "$MEM_PATH"'
tasks.block_on: Missing memory entry path, schema validation failure, or unfilled TBD placeholders

on_failure.message: Author a type:bug memory entry capturing this fix's root cause, symptoms, and affected files. Set meta.memory_entry in spec.md or export MEMORY_ENTRY_PATH before re-running. Schema rules at .claude/skills/memory-schema-enforcer/validate.py.
on_failure.action: Create/fix the memory entry, then re-run /implement

tasks.plan_ref: /tasks step 6.8 (FR-006/FR-010); tasks-verify-templates.json conditional_gates T-MEM-CAPTURE
tasks.note: depends_on:['T-VERIFY-SMOKE'] is preserved verbatim from the template; in this composite-gate layout the smoke check lives inside T-FINAL.composed_of, so the effective predecessor is T-DOC-GATE (see gate field). on_failure message/action preserved verbatim.

tasks.description: Composite final gate — orchestrates all verification checks before merge. Conditional checks evaluated against this spec's critical_requirements (type=bugfix, portal=app, ui_changes=minor, ui_complexity=low, database_changes=false, routes=[]). Per /tasks step 7, individual T-VERIFY-* tasks are NOT emitted — this composite replaces them.
tasks.verify: All composed_of checks marked PASS or SKIP-WITH-REASON; no FAIL
tasks.block_on: Any check with blocks_merge=true marked FAIL
tasks.plan_ref: /tasks step 7; .specify/templates/tasks-verify-templates.json final_gate

composed_of.skip_reason: Repo is vanilla JS (Electron main + renderer, CommonJS); no TS build / no tsc step (plan Technical Context). The TS files under contracts/ are validated separately via `npx tsc --noEmit --strict --skipLibCheck` (Phase 0.3 gate, already exit 0) — that is a design-artifact check, not a build typecheck.

composed_of.note: eslint --ext .ts,.tsx,.js . is configured via npm script, but no project-level .eslintrc was found at repo root (only functions/.eslintrc.js). Best-effort — fix any errors on the touched files; flat-config absence may make this a no-op, in which case mark SKIP-WITH-REASON.

Warn-only. Check: bracketed [stt-lifecycle] logging tag, {success,error,dropped?} IPC shape, single-live-session-per-side (no failover D1), one monotonic generation reused (not two), and the mandatory !(1000 && 'client') reconnect filter (D2) if Phase 3 shipped.

composed_of.command: node --test src/features/listen/stt/__tests__/sttService-lifecycle.test.js  (plus the existing suite via `npm test`)
composed_of.note: No `npm run test:unit` exists. The new AC-1 (always) and AC-2/3/4/5 (for whichever gated phases shipped) live in sttService-lifecycle.test.js; existing tests run via `npm test` (node --test src/**/__tests__/**/*.test.js).

composed_of.skip_reason: No separate integration suite / `npm run test:integration` and no test DB or containers in this Electron app (research.md §4 Distribution: Integration 0%). The cross-module behavior (renderer↔main generation handshake, renewal partial-failure, reconnect swap) is covered by the mocked node:test units (AC-2/3/4) using a scripted fake createSTT socket — there is no live-socket integration tier here.

composed_of.command: Manual: run `npm start`, execute quickstart AC-6 (Start→speak→mash Start/Stop ~10× ⇒ ZERO 'session not active' lines, no orphan warnings, no dup/garbled transcript). This is the headline critical path.
composed_of.note: No `npm run test:smoke` / Playwright for this desktop app. Smoke is the manual Windows+Deepgram AC-6 (and AC-7 if Phase 4 shipped). See spec §10 / quickstart §4.

composed_of.skip_reason: database_changes=false. No SQLite schema change, no migration, no src/database/schema.orm touch (plan Phase 0.5 SKIPPED; data-model 'Zero persisted schema changes'). The `generation` field is an additive in-memory/IPC value, not persisted.

No HTTP API / REST routes (routes=[]; portal=app). The only contracts are TypeScript design artifacts in specs/.../contracts/ validated via `npx tsc --noEmit` (Phase 0.3 gate, exit 0) — there is no `npm run test:contract` HTTP contract suite. The audio IPC `generation` field is additive and backward-compatible (EDGE_CASES E15).

composed_of.command: Manual UI smoke — confirm the 'Reconnecting…'/'Reconnected' status text renders via the existing update-status channel, and (on reconnect exhaustion) a one-click 'Resume' affordance appears and performs Stop→Listen. Header (MainHeader.js) unchanged.
composed_of.note: Required because ui_changes=minor (FR-3.4). Only meaningful if Phase 3 shipped; if Phase 3 was gated out, mark SKIP-WITH-REASON (no UI surface changed). No `/verify-ui` automation harness wired for this Electron app — manual verification.

composed_of.skip_reason: E2E FORBIDDEN here (research.md §4 gate): a live Deepgram STT socket lifecycle is not deterministically CI-automatable and Deepgram is a paid (MEDIUM-risk) API. The equivalent coverage is the MANUAL smoke AC-6/7/8 (quickstart §4). No `npm run test:e2e` / browser harness for this desktop app.

ui_complexity=low (one status-line string + a one-click Resume button reusing the existing channel); no accessibility-specific keywords in spec; no moderate/major UI surface. No `npm run a11y:lighthouse` configured.

composed_of.command: Verify: (a) the guarded-send drop path (FR-1.1) cannot mask a genuine 'sessions never came up' failure silently — the FR-0.3 throttled counter + FR-3.4 status keep it visible; (b) reconnect (if shipped) reuses the same provider/model/handlers/config and never logs API-key fragments in status/log lines; (c) the additive `generation` IPC field is not attacker-influenceable across renderer contexts (single-user desktop, in-process); (d) no new IPC channel widens the attack surface (FR-3.4 reuses update-status).

Review the diff for correctness: ONE monotonic generation concept reused by FR-0.2/FR-2.4 (not two); guarded send mirrors the macOS :687/:699 skip-don't-crash exactly; the reconnect close-filter is !(code===1000 && reason==='client') in BOTH onclose and onerror (D2 — the headline memory lesson); reconnect swap is atomic (assign new, then close dead — no transient null, INV-1); renewal reschedule re-arms in finally/catch (not after the throw point); listenCapture audio-graph wiring is byte-identical (ownership-only refactor); macOS native loop + Linux mic-only paths not regressed (D3, E11/E12).
